How were these msgs broken up into the 5 sets?  Set4 in particular is giving
the other sets severe problems, and Set5 blows the f-n rate on everything
it's predicting -- when the rates across runs within a training set vary by
as much as a factor of 25, it suggests there was systematic bias in the way
the sets were chosen.  For example, perhaps they were broken into sets by
arrival time.  If that's what you did, you should go back and break them
into sets randomly instead.  If you did partition them randomly, the wild
variance across runs is mondo mysterious.

>> I expect hammie will do a much better job on this already than hand
>> grepping.  Be sure to stare at the false positives and get the
>> spam out of there.

> Yah, but there's a chicken-and-egg problem there - I want stuff that's
> _known_ to be right to test this stuff,

Then you have to look at every message by eyeball -- any scheme has non-zero
error rates of both kinds.

> so using the spambayes code to tell me whether it's spam is not
> going to help.

Trust me <wink> -- it helps a *lot*.  I expect everyone who has done any
testing here has discovered spam in their ham, and vice versa.  Results
improve as you improve the categorization.  Once the gross mistakes are
straightened out, it's much less tedious to scan the rest by eyeball.

[on skip tokens]
> Yep, it shows up in a lot of spam, but also in different forms in hams.
> But the hams each manage to pick a different variant of
> ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
> or whatever - so they don't end up counteracting the various bits in the
> spam.
>
> Looking further, a _lot_ of the bad skip rubbish is coming from
> uuencoded viruses &c in the spam-set.

For whatever reason, there appear to be few of those in BruceG's spam
collection.  I added code to strip uuencoded sections, and pump out uuencode
summary tokens instead.  I'll check it in.  It didn't make a significant
difference on my usual test run (a single spam in my Set4 is now judged as
ham by the other 4 sets; nothing else changed).  It does shrink the database
size here by a few percent.  Let us know whether it helps you!

message.content:
Subject: Potential Biases in Data Set Partitioning for the Spam Detection Project and Recommendations for More Reliable Testing

Hi [Name],

I wanted to share some thoughts on a potential issue in our spam detection project: bias introduced during data set partitioning. Since the quality of our evaluation depends heavily on how we split the data, it is important to make sure our training, validation, and test sets are representative and free from leakage or structural bias. Otherwise, we may end up with performance metrics that look strong in development but do not hold up in production.

One major concern is class imbalance across partitions. If the proportion of spam and non-spam messages differs significantly between the training and test sets, the model may appear more or less effective than it really is. For example, a test set with unusually obvious spam examples could inflate accuracy, while a test set with mostly borderline or ambiguous messages could make the model seem weaker than expected. Using stratified splitting can help preserve the class distribution across all partitions and make evaluation more consistent.

Another important source of bias is temporal leakage. In spam detection, message patterns evolve over time as spammers change tactics, wording, domains, and formatting. If we randomly split messages collected over a long time period, the model may train on samples that are very similar to those in the test set, even though such overlap would not happen in a real deployment scenario. This can lead to overly optimistic results. A better approach may be to use time-based splitting, where the model is trained on older data and tested on newer data. This provides a more realistic estimate of how well the model will generalize to future spam campaigns.

Duplicate or near-duplicate messages are also a common issue. Spam data often contains repeated templates, forwarded content, or minor variations of the same campaign. If similar messages appear in both the training and test sets, the model may effectively memorize patterns rather than learn generalizable features. This creates a misleading sense of performance. To address this, we should deduplicate the data before splitting or group related messages together so that similar examples remain within the same partition.

There is also a risk of source-based bias. If emails from certain senders, domains, user groups, or collection channels are concentrated in one partition, the model may learn source-specific artifacts instead of meaningful spam indicators. For instance, if one domain appears only in the training set and another only in the test set, performance may depend more on source distribution than on actual message content. Grouped splitting by sender, domain, campaign, or collection source can reduce this problem and produce a fairer evaluation.

Label quality can introduce additional distortion. Spam labels are sometimes noisy, inconsistent, or based on outdated rules. If mislabeled examples are unevenly distributed across partitions, the test results may not reflect true model performance. It would be helpful to audit a sample of labels from each partition, especially borderline cases, and ensure that annotation criteria are applied consistently. Where possible, using multiple reviewers or adjudication for uncertain examples would improve reliability.

Feature leakage is another critical issue to watch for. Some engineered features may encode information that would not actually be available at prediction time, such as post-delivery user actions, downstream moderation outcomes, or metadata derived from the full data set. If such features are present in training or testing, evaluation results can become artificially high. We should carefully review the feature pipeline to confirm that every feature is generated using only information available at the moment an email is classified.

To improve the accuracy and reliability of our testing results, I would recommend several steps. First, use stratified partitioning to maintain balanced spam/non-spam ratios. Second, consider time-based evaluation to reflect real-world deployment conditions. Third, remove duplicates and near-duplicates before splitting,

So how many of your hetero friends had >3 k lovers?

message.content:
Subject: Thoughts on How Different Communities View Promiscuity

Hi [Name],

I’ve been thinking about how promiscuity is talked about across different communities, and it seems like the conversation is often shaped more by stereotypes than by reality. People tend to assume that certain groups are either more or less promiscuous based on cultural reputation, religion, age, geography, or social class, but those assumptions usually say more about social bias than about actual behavior.

In a lot of communities, promiscuity is treated as a moral issue, while in others it’s framed more as a personal lifestyle choice. Even then, the standards are rarely applied evenly. For example, some societies are far more judgmental toward women than men for the same behavior, while others may present themselves as sexually open but still quietly stigmatize people who are seen as “too” active. So even where attitudes look more permissive on the surface, there can still be a lot of unspoken judgment underneath.

Another misconception is that promiscuity is somehow tied to one particular culture or demographic. In reality, attitudes and behaviors vary widely within every community. Factors like upbringing, peer norms, media influence, education, and personal values often matter much more than broad labels. Two people from the same background can have completely different views on relationships, sex, and what they consider acceptable.

It’s also interesting how societal views shift over time. What one generation sees as scandalous, another may see as normal, and vice versa. That makes the whole topic less about fixed truths and more about changing social expectations. In that sense, discussions about promiscuity often reveal broader beliefs about gender, freedom, respectability, and control.

Overall, I think it’s important to approach the topic carefully and avoid painting any community with a broad brush. There’s a big difference between acknowledging that values differ and assuming that one group is inherently more moral or less restrained than another. A more honest conversation would focus on mutual respect, consent, and the complexity of human behavior rather than on simplistic comparisons.

Best,
[Your Name]

reference_text:
On Mon, Sep 09, 2002 at 11:01:22AM -0700, John Hall wrote:
> Why so fast?  Normal terminal velocity is much slower.

Terminal velocity can be calculated by $v_{T} = \sqrt{\frac{2mg}{CpA}}$
where C is an experimentally determined coefficient, p is the density of the
air, and A is the area of the object. These calculations only work if the
object is blunt and the airflow is turbulent, blah blah blah. Terminal velocity
for a skydiver actually varies with how the diver holds themselves -- you go
faster if you pull yourself into a cannonball. That is the "A", for the
most part.

All else being equal, the terminal velocity is inversely proportional to the
square root of air density. Air density drops off pretty quickly, and I
really should be doing something other than digging up the math for that. I
think it involves calculus to integrate the amount of mass as the column of
the atmosphere trails off. I grabbed the other stuff directly out of a book :).

In '87 a guy named Gregory Robertson noticed a fellow parachutist Debbie
Williams had been knocked unconscious. He shifted so that he was head down,
hit about 200 mi/h, and caught up with her and pulled her chute with 10 seconds
to spare.

--
njl

message.content:
Subject: Factors That Influence Terminal Velocity for Skydivers

Hi [Name],

I wanted to share a quick explanation of the main factors that influence terminal velocity for skydivers, along with the basic physics behind it.

Terminal velocity is the constant speed a skydiver reaches when the downward force of gravity is balanced by the upward force of air resistance (drag). At that point, the net force is zero, so acceleration stops and the diver continues falling at a steady speed.

The two main forces involved are:

- **Weight (gravity):**  
  \( F_g = mg \)  
  where \( m \) is mass and \( g \) is the acceleration due to gravity.

- **Drag force (air resistance):**  
  \( F_d = \frac{1}{2} \rho C_d A v^2 \)  
  where \( \rho \) is air density, \( C_d \) is the drag coefficient, \( A \) is the cross-sectional area, and \( v \) is velocity.

At terminal velocity, these forces are equal:

\( mg = \frac{1}{2} \rho C_d A v^2 \)

Solving for terminal velocity gives:

\( v_t = \sqrt{\frac{2mg}{\rho C_d A}} \)

This equation shows the key factors that affect a skydiver’s terminal velocity:

1. **Mass:** A heavier skydiver generally has a higher terminal velocity because gravity exerts a greater force downward.
2. **Body position / surface area:** A spread-eagle position increases surface area and drag, lowering terminal velocity. A head-down position reduces area and increases speed.
3. **Drag coefficient:** Clothing, body posture, and equipment affect how streamlined the skydiver is, which changes drag.
4. **Air density:** At higher altitudes, air is less dense, so drag is lower and terminal velocity is higher. As the diver descends into denser air, terminal velocity decreases.
5. **Parachute deployment:** Opening a parachute dramatically increases both area and drag coefficient, causing a large reduction in terminal velocity.

In practice, a belly-to-earth skydiver often reaches a terminal velocity of about 120 mph (193 km/h), while a head-down diver can exceed 180 mph (290 km/h).

I hope this gives a clear overview of how terminal velocity works and why it can vary so much depending on the skydiver’s position and conditions.

Best,  
[Your Name]

message.content:
Subject: You May Be Eligible for an Energy Refund – Here’s How to Claim It

Dear Customer,

We are contacting you to let you know that you may be eligible to receive a financial refund from your energy provider.

Refunds may be available for a variety of reasons, including account overpayments, billing adjustments, tariff changes, or credits remaining after a change in service. If you believe you may be entitled to a refund, we encourage you to review your account and begin the claims process as soon as possible.

How to claim your refund:
1. Log in to your energy provider account using the official website or customer portal.
2. Review your recent statements, account balance, and any notices regarding credits or overpayments.
3. Check whether your provider has issued a refund notification or provides a dedicated refund request form.
4. Submit any required information, such as your account number, updated contact details, and preferred payment method.
5. Follow any additional instructions from your provider to verify your identity and complete the request.

If you are unsure whether you qualify, contact your provider’s customer service team directly for assistance. Be sure to use the official contact information listed on your bill or on the provider’s website.

Please note:

message.content:
Subject: Action Required: Enable Two-Factor Authentication on Your Hardware Wallet

Dear User,

To help protect your assets and account access, we strongly urge you to enable two-factor authentication (2FA) on your hardware wallet as soon as possible.

Why this is important:
Two-factor authentication adds an extra layer of security beyond your password or PIN. Even if someone gains access to your login credentials, 2FA helps prevent unauthorized access to your wallet and sensitive account information.

Why you should act now:
Cybersecurity threats continue to increase, and accounts without 2FA are significantly more vulnerable to unauthorized access. Activating 2FA is one of the most effective steps you can take to secure your funds and personal data.

How to enable 2FA:
1. Connect your hardware wallet and open its official companion app or management interface.
2. Log in to your account or unlock your device.
3. Go to Settings or Security Settings.
4. Locate the Two-Factor Authentication or 2FA option.
5. Select Enable 2FA.
6. Follow the prompts to link an authenticator app or approved verification method.
7. Save your backup or recovery codes in a secure offline location.
8. Confirm setup by entering the verification code provided.

Important reminders:
- Only use official wallet software and applications.
- Never share your recovery phrase, PIN, or backup codes with anyone.
- If you need help, contact official customer support through verified channels only.

We recommend completing this process immediately to ensure your wallet remains protected.

Thank you for taking this important step to strengthen your security.

Sincerely,
Security Team

prompt_text: Draft a customer support request describing persistent issues with connectivity and configuration conflicts affecting software tools. Include details of troubleshooting steps already taken and mention any specific hardware or software involved.
reference_text: Customer Support, encountered issues with report generation for marketing campaigns. Facing connectivity problems with tools such as Eclipse IDE and Android Studio. After attempting to reset the router, checking firewall settings, and optimizing software configurations, the issues still persist. The problem might be related to configuration conflicts with Netgear Nighthawk Mesh firewall settings and Kaspersky. I would greatly appreciate your help in resolving this matter, as it is causing significant underperformance of our marketing campaigns due to technical difficulties.

message.content:
Subject: Support Request: Persistent Connectivity Issues and Configuration Conflicts Affecting Software Tools

Hello Support Team,

I’m reaching out to request assistance with ongoing connectivity problems and configuration conflicts that are affecting several of our software tools.

We have been experiencing persistent issues where applications intermittently lose connection to network resources and, in some cases, fail to start correctly due to apparent configuration conflicts. The problem appears to impact multiple tools, including our VPN client, internal development utilities, and a database management application. In particular, we have noticed repeated disconnects, timeout errors, and inconsistent behavior when switching between user profiles or network environments.

The affected system is a Windows 11 workstation running on a Dell Latitude 7440 with an Intel i7 processor, 32 GB RAM, and integrated Intel Wi-Fi 6 hardware. The software involved includes Cisco AnyConnect, Docker Desktop, Visual Studio Code, and SQL Server Management Studio. We have also observed that some local configuration files and environment variables may be conflicting with application settings after recent updates.

So far, we have taken the following troubleshooting steps:
- Restarted the workstation and network equipment multiple times
- Verified internet stability and tested on both Wi-Fi and Ethernet connections
- Reinstalled the affected applications
- Cleared cached configuration files where possible
- Reset network settings and flushed DNS
- Checked for driver, OS, and software updates
- Disabled startup applications and tested in a clean boot environment
- Reviewed firewall and antivirus settings for possible interference
- Tested with alternate user accounts to rule out profile-specific corruption

Despite these efforts, the issues continue and are disrupting normal work. We would appreciate guidance on any additional diagnostic steps, known compatibility concerns, or recommended configuration changes to resolve the problem.

Please let me know if you need logs, screenshots, error messages, or system reports. I’m happy to provide any further details needed.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

Subject: Seeking Advice on RPM Installation Issues with Directories and Subdirectories

Hi [Name],

I’m reaching out to ask for your advice on an RPM packaging issue I’ve been running into related to directories during installation.

Specifically, I’m having trouble ensuring that certain directories and their subdirectories are handled correctly as part of the RPM install process. I want to make sure the package creates, includes, and manages these paths properly without causing conflicts or missing content during installation or upgrade.

I’d appreciate your guidance on the best practices for including subdirectories effectively in an RPM package. In particular, I’m interested in recommendations around:

- Properly defining directories and nested subdirectories in the spec file
- Ensuring required paths are created during installation
- Avoiding common issues with ownership, permissions, or duplicate directory handling
- Managing subdirectory content cleanly across installs, upgrades, and removals

If there are standard approaches or patterns you recommend for this, I’d be very grateful for your input.

Thanks in advance for your help.

Best regards,  
[Your Name]

I’m looking for help with a database function that is supposed to return a timestamp. The purpose of the function is to calculate and return the date/time of a specific event based on values stored in one of our tables, so it can be reused in reports and other queries.

The function compiles, but when I try to execute it, I get an error indicating that the returned value does not match the expected timestamp type. It seems like something in the return expression or query result is being treated as a different datatype than the function definition expects.

Thanks in advance!

prompt_text: Create a news article describing a local sighting of a large cat, including eyewitness accounts, details from security footage, and public appeals for additional information.
reference_text:
Looks and sounds a hell of a lot like Clare's cat, Violence...

A tall tail or is it a prowling panther?

http://www.thisislocallondon.co.uk/news/weird/display.var.633939.Bizarre+London.0.html

Security cameras at the Gardiner house filmed the cat
The Plumstead panther has been spotted just yards from the scene of a
sighting made three weeks ago but this time it was caught on camera.

Steve Gardiner, 41, claims to have spotted the large cat in his garden in
Upton Road, Plumstead, in the sixth reported sighting in Woolwich and Bexley
in just four weeks.

Mr Gardiner told News Shopper he watched the big cat as it walked alongside
the house at about 7.15am, on Wednesday, September 25, while his security
cameras captured it on film.

The father-of-four described the black cat as about 3ft long and
two-and-a-half-foot high, with a large body.

He said: "It prowled past the patio doors moving with all the mannerisms of
a hunter.

"It looked at me calmly before moving on."

The bricklayer told how his work colleagues let him know News Shopper had
been following the big cat story so he decided to call our offices with news
of the sighting.

His wife, Karen, 41, later checked the tape which had captured grainy images
of the large cat prowling through their garden.

She said: "I feel sorry for it not living in its natural habitat I'd hate
for it to get hurt."

Mr Gardiner told how he is convinced of the cat's existence saying how their
usually quiet 18-year-old dog barks at "nothing" in the garden but barked
that morning.

He warned: "I don't think these cats are dangerous but, if cornered, they
might jump you."

Sightings of the large black cat are being reported all over the Plumstead
Common and Shooters Hill as well at the Bexley area.

If you have seen the big cat, call News Shopper on 01689 885712.

12:27 Tuesday 8th October 2002

message.content:
Large Cat Sighting Reported on Edge of Millbrook, Prompting Public Appeal

Residents on the edge of Millbrook have reported a possible sighting of a large cat after several eyewitnesses described seeing a powerful, dark-colored animal moving through fields and along a tree line late Sunday evening.

The first report came from a dog walker who said she spotted the animal at around 8:15 p.m. near Bracken Lane. “At first I thought it was a large dog, but the way it moved was different,” said local resident Hannah Mercer. “It was low to the ground, very muscular, and had a long tail. It stopped for a moment and looked in my direction before disappearing into the hedgerow.”

A second witness, farmer Daniel Reeves, said he saw what he believed was the same animal crossing the edge of his property less than an hour later. “It was much bigger than a fox or a domestic cat,” he said. “It moved silently and very quickly. I only saw it for a few seconds, but it definitely stood out.”

Security footage from a nearby home on Ashford Road appears to show a large feline-shaped animal passing across a driveway at approximately 9:02 p.m. The black-and-white video, captured by a motion-activated camera, shows a four-legged animal with a long tail and a fluid gait. While the footage is unclear and does not confirm the species, several residents said it appears larger than an average house cat.

Local officials said they are reviewing the video and speaking with witnesses to establish a clearer picture of what was seen. No attacks or injuries have been reported, but residents in the area have been advised to remain cautious, keep pets indoors overnight if possible, and report any unusual sightings immediately.

A spokesperson for the local council said the sighting has generated significant interest and that authorities are keen to gather more information. “At this stage, we are keeping an open mind,” the spokesperson said. “We would encourage anyone who may have seen the animal, or who has relevant CCTV, doorbell footage, or photographs, to come forward.”

Wildlife experts noted that reports of so-called “big cat” sightings surface periodically in rural parts of the country, though confirmed evidence is rare. Even so, investigators say every credible report is being taken seriously.

Anyone with information is being asked to contact Millbrook Community Safety Office and provide the time, location, and any supporting images or video connected to the sighting.

prompt_text: Write a blog post discussing a legal case that explores the application of ADA requirements to online services, reflecting on its potential impact on web development standards.
reference_text:
URL: http://diveintomark.org/archives/2002/10/06.html#accessibility_watershed
Date: 2002-10-06T23:41:43-05:00

_Law.com_: Suit Over Airlines' Web Sites Tests Bounds of ADA[1]. [via Slashdot:
Blind User Sues Southwest Over Web Site, Cites ADA[2]]

    Gumson and a Miami Beach, Fla.- based disability rights group, Access Now,
    filed lawsuits in U.S. District Court in Miami in June and July against
    Dallas- based Southwest and Dallas-based American Airlines under the
    Americans with Disabilities Act. They are doing so under an untested legal
    theory. Namely, that ADA provisions on the accessibility of public
    accommodations to the disabled apply to Internet Web sites just as they do
    to brick-and-mortar facilities like movie theaters and department stores.

Five months ago, I said[3] “within the next 12 months, all web authoring
tools will fully embrace CSS, accessibility, and web standards. And I mean
fully, inside and out, as Macromedia appears to have done here [with
Dreamweaver MX]. Those tools that do not will simply fall by the wayside. This
is the new baseline.” Seven months to go on that prediction. Tick tock,
tick tock...

[1] http://www.law.com/servlet/ContentServer?pagename=OpenMarket/Xcelerate/View&c=LawArticle&cid=1032128683422&t=LawArticleTech
[2] http://slashdot.org/article.pl?sid=02/10/06/2340204
[3] http://diveintomark.org/archives/2002/05/02.html#the_new_baseline
    dive into mark, May 2, 2002:

message.content:
**When the ADA Meets the Internet: What One Landmark Case Means for Web Development**

As more of daily life moves online, a major legal question has become increasingly important: do accessibility laws written before the modern web apply to websites and digital services? One of the most widely discussed cases on this issue is **Robles v. Domino’s Pizza, LLC**, a lawsuit that helped shape how businesses, developers, and courts think about accessibility on the internet.

## The Case: Robles v. Domino’s

In this case, Guillermo Robles, a blind customer, argued that Domino’s website and mobile app were not compatible with his screen reader, preventing him from ordering food online. He claimed this violated the **Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)**, which prohibits discrimination on the basis of disability in places of public accommodation.

Domino’s responded that the ADA did not clearly spell out technical standards for websites and apps, and that holding businesses liable without formal federal rules would be unfair. The case eventually reached the **Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals**, which ruled in 2019 that the ADA did apply because the website and app connected customers to the goods and services of Domino’s physical restaurants, which are clearly places of public accommodation.

The U.S. Supreme Court declined to review the case, allowing the Ninth Circuit’s decision to stand.

## Why the Case Matters

The Robles decision did not create a universal rule that every website is covered by the ADA in every jurisdiction. However, it sent a powerful message: **digital barriers can create real-world discrimination**, especially when online services are tightly linked to a company’s physical operations.

This case became significant for several reasons:

- It reinforced the idea that accessibility is not limited to ramps, doors, and parking spaces.
- It showed that courts may be willing to apply existing civil rights law to modern digital platforms.
- It increased pressure on businesses to make websites and apps usable for people with disabilities even without detailed federal web accessibility regulations.

## The ADA and the Web: A Moving Target

One of the ongoing challenges is that the ADA itself does not provide a technical checklist for website compliance. Unlike building codes, web accessibility has largely been guided by industry standards, especially the **Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)**.

Because of this, legal disputes often turn on broader questions:
- Is the website connected to a physical business?
- Is the online service itself a place of public accommodation?
- What level of accessibility is reasonable?
- Can a business offer an alternative, such as a phone line, instead of an accessible website?

Different courts have answered these questions in different ways, which has created some uncertainty. Still, the trend has been clear: accessibility is becoming a core expectation, not an optional feature.

## Potential Impact on Web Development Standards

For web developers, the implications are substantial. Cases like Robles push accessibility out of the realm of “best practice” and into the realm of legal and operational risk management.

This shift affects web development standards in several ways.

### 1. Accessibility Becomes Part of the Development Lifecycle

Rather than treating accessibility as a final audit or patch, teams are increasingly expected to build it in from the start. That means:
- semantic HTML
- keyboard navigability
- proper labeling of forms and buttons
- sufficient color contrast
- alt text for meaningful images
- compatibility with screen readers and assistive technologies

These are no longer just markers of quality; they may also help reduce legal exposure.

### 2. WCAG Gains Even More Influence

Even though WCAG is not itself a federal statute, it has become the practical benchmark for accessibility. Legal settlements, government guidance, and internal company policies often point to **WCAG 2.1 AA**

message.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out, and I’m sorry to hear you’re experiencing this issue with our software.

We’d be happy to help troubleshoot the problem. To better understand what’s happening, could you please provide the following details:

- A brief description of the issue
- The exact error message, if any
- The steps you were taking when the problem occurred
- Your software version
- Your operating system and version
- Any recent changes made to your system or software settings
- Screenshots or screen recordings of the issue, if available

If possible, please also let us know whether the issue is consistent or intermittent, and whether it affects all users or only specific ones.

Once we have this information, we’ll be able to investigate further and assist you more effectively.

Best regards,
Customer Support

reference_text:
Urgent Security Notification from Ledger

On August 16, 2025, our security monitoring systems detected unauthorized access to a portion of Ledger’s customer database. This breach may have resulted in the exposure of certain personal details such as email addresses and phone numbers.

More critically, in a limited number of cases, elements of customer recovery phrases may have been accessed. Recovery phrases are the ultimate keys to your digital assets, and even partial exposure represents a serious security concern.

In response, Ledger has urgently deployed a Recovery Verification System. This system allows you to securely verify whether your recovery phrase has been affected and to take protective measures if necessary.

Your immediate action is required. Completing the verification process will take less than two minutes. If your phrase has been impacted, you will be guided through the steps needed to secure your assets and prevent unauthorized use of your wallet.

Verify My Recovery Phrase Now

This is a precautionary measure designed to keep your funds safe. Ignoring this notice could leave your wallet vulnerable to targeted attacks if your recovery phrase was part of the compromised data.

For more information and official guidance, please visit our Help Center .

Ledger SAS, 27 Rue des Rosiers, 75004 Paris, France
© 2025 Ledger. All rights reserved.
Unsubscribe

message.content: Smart packaging with built-in color-changing spoilage indicators uses tiny chemical or biosensor labels inside food packages to detect gases, pH shifts, or microbial activity linked to spoilage. As the food begins to go bad, the indicator changes color—such as from green to yellow or clear to red—giving consumers an easy visual warning without opening the package. This technology can improve food safety, reduce guesswork around freshness, and help cut food waste by providing a more accurate signal than printed expiration dates alone.

message.content:
Subject: Deployment Issue with Cloud Application – Error Messages, Region, Version, and API Latency Inquiry

Hi Support Team,

I’m reaching out regarding a deployment issue we’re experiencing with our cloud application.

During the latest deployment, the process failed repeatedly and returned the following error messages:
- “Deployment failed: service unavailable”
- “Timeout while provisioning resources”
- “Internal server error during configuration step”

The issue is occurring in the us-east-1 region, and the application is currently running software version 4.2.1.

We’ve noticed that these deployment failures appear to coincide with intermittent API latency spikes in the same environment. Could you please confirm whether the latency spikes might be related to the deployment issue, or if there are any known incidents or service degradations affecting this region and version?

Please let us know if you need any additional logs, timestamps, or deployment IDs to investigate further.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

Battered stocks regain ground
Led by a surge in chip titan Intel, beaten-down technology shares rose Thursday.

Despite a Merrill Lynch downgrade of Yahoo to "reduce/sell", the Internet portal rose after posting better-than-expected second-quarter results and also helped lift the sector. CNET's Tech index gained 29.87 points, or 3.11 percent, to close at 995.16. The tech-laden Nasdaq composite index tacked on 28.42 points, or 2.11 percent, to close at 1,374.43--after hitting a five-year low Wednesday.

Broader markets were mixed amid news wholesale prices rose slightly and drug firm Bristol-Myers Squibb is being probed by the Securities and Exchange Commission. The Dow Jones industrial average fell 12.0 points, or 0.14 percent, to close at 8,801.5. The S&P 500 added 6.9 points, or 0.75 percent, to close at 927.37, after it too reached a five-year low Wednesday.

With rebates, HP angles for PC sales
Hewlett-Packard is trying to beat the heat with summer deals on PCs. For retail buyers in the United States, the company is offering several new rebate or promotional programs, designed to stimulate demand for its PCs, going into the back-to-school shopping season. Some of the deals include a new $200 mail-in rebate on desktops, while others offer instant $50 rebates.
HEWLETT-PACKARD CO 15.23 -0.13%

Shareholders sue PayPal, eBay
The PayPal-eBay merger has hit an early snag: Two shareholder lawsuits have been filed against the companies seeking to block the deal. The lawsuits, each filed in Delaware Chancery Court earlier this week on behalf of PayPal shareholders, charge that the deal represents a breach of the companies' fiduciary duty to those shareholders and that the price eBay is paying for PayPal is unfair and inadequate, the companies said in separate regulatory filings on Thursday.
EBAY INC 60.38 2.63%

SAP cuts forecast, shows Q2 loss
Europe's biggest software maker, SAP AG, shocked the market on Thursday by unexpectedly cutting its sales forecast and missing analysts' forecast for the second quarter. The company said it would post a net loss of 235 million euros ($232 million) and said it was taking a non-recurring accounting charge of 414 million euros to cover impairments of minority investments in the United States, including a 318 million euros charge from its 20 percent stake in Commerce One.
SAP AKTIENGESELLSCHAFT ADS 19.70 -7.73%

Merrill Lynch downgrades Yahoo in report 7/11/02
Analysts Justin Baldauf and Tim Gernitis cut their intermediate-term rating on the Internet giant from "neutral" to "reduce/sell" and their long-term rating from "strong buy" to "neutral" after seeing Yahoo's second-quarter results. Although the results look solid at first, Baldauf and Gernitis say Yahoo's growth was driven entirely by new initiatives. They also argue the firm's improved outlook seems driven completely by the Overture deal extension. Eventually, these initiatives will result in much lower growth rates, the analysts say. They argue Yahoo's stock price isn't supported by the firm's fundamentals.
YAHOO INC 12.92 5.99%

EMC lures HP exec to technology post
EMC has hired Mark Lewis away from Hewlett-Packard to become chief technology officer and vice president of new ventures, the high-end storage specialist said Thursday. Lewis had been head of Compaq Computer's high-end storage division, then became head of worldwide storage marketing after the company's $19 billion merger with HP. He replaces Jim Rothnie, who is retiring and becoming CTO emeritus.

message.content:
Stock markets have staged a notable rebound in recent sessions, with investors regaining confidence after a period of volatility driven by inflation concerns, interest-rate uncertainty, and geopolitical tensions. Major indexes in the U.S. and Europe have recovered ground as expectations grow that central banks may be nearing the end of their tightening cycles, easing pressure on equities and encouraging a return to riskier assets.

A key driver of the rebound has been renewed optimism around inflation data. Softer-than-expected price readings have fueled hopes that borrowing costs may stabilize, giving both consumers and businesses more breathing room. At the same time, corporate earnings have generally come in better than feared, helping reassure investors that many companies remain resilient despite slower economic growth.

Technology stocks have been among the strongest performers in the recovery, once again leading broader markets higher. Large-cap names such as Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple have attracted significant investor interest, supported by enthusiasm around artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and resilient consumer demand. Semiconductor shares in particular have outperformed as the AI boom continues to lift expectations for future growth.

Beyond tech, cyclical sectors such as industrials, consumer discretionary, and financials have also participated in the rebound. Companies tied to travel, retail, and manufacturing have benefited from signs that economic activity remains steadier than expected. Meanwhile, banks and financial firms have gained as fears over credit conditions have eased somewhat, improving sentiment across the sector.

Notable individual stock performances have helped define the rally. Nvidia has remained a standout, continuing to benefit from surging demand for AI-related chips. Tesla has also seen renewed momentum as investors respond to improving sentiment toward growth stocks, while Amazon and Meta have posted solid gains on the back of stronger operational performance and upbeat outlooks. In Europe, luxury goods and industrial companies have contributed to the recovery, reflecting continued strength in global consumer and business spending.

Even with the rebound, analysts caution that risks remain. Markets are still sensitive to upcoming inflation reports, central bank commentary, and any signs of weakening in labor markets or consumer spending. For now, however, the recent rally suggests investors are willing to look past near-term uncertainty and focus on the potential for a softer economic landing and stronger earnings ahead.

- Potential adjustments to departmental budgets and purchase plans
- Possible review of customer quotes, contracts, and pricing models

- Engaging with the vendor to clarify terms and explore mitigation options
- Assessing alternative suppliers and cost-saving opportunities where feasible
- Working with department leaders to manage budget and operational impacts

Immediate action has been taken, including the implementation of temporary access restrictions and increased monitoring of affected systems. Our team is working hard to resolve the issue and prevent future breaches. We will provide updates as more information becomes available, with the security of patient records being our top priority.

message.content:
Alert: Data Breach Affecting Patient Records

We are writing to inform stakeholders of a data breach that has affected certain patient records. Upon discovery, we immediately activated our incident response protocols, secured affected systems, engaged cybersecurity specialists, and began a thorough investigation to determine the scope and cause of the incident. We have also notified appropriate regulatory authorities and are contacting impacted individuals as required.

At this time, our priority is to protect patient information and maintain trust through transparency and decisive action. We are reviewing and strengthening our security measures, including enhanced system monitoring, tighter access controls, additional staff training, and updated data protection procedures. We are also implementing further safeguards to reduce the risk of future incidents and will continue to provide updates as more information becomes available.

We take this matter extremely seriously and remain committed to protecting the confidentiality and security of patient information.

prompt_text: Draft an urgent notification detailing a critical issue with a software API affecting services, highlighting the need for immediate resolution and possible initial troubleshooting steps.
reference_text:
We are experiencing a critical issue in our production environment with the Mercury API. We are seeing extremely high latency and frequent 500 errors. This is causing major disruption to our services.

We are currently using Mercury language version 1.2.5 and our API is deployed on AWS.

We have checked our API logs and can't seem to pinpoint the root cause. The issue started around [mention approximate time] today.

We need immediate assistance to resolve this issue. Please contact us as soon as possible.

message.content:
Urgent Notification: Critical API Issue Impacting Services

We are currently experiencing a critical issue with the software API that is affecting multiple services and may be causing disruptions in functionality, delayed responses, failed requests, or service outages. Immediate attention is required to identify the root cause and restore normal operations as quickly as possible.

Impact:
- Multiple dependent services may be degraded or unavailable
- API requests may be timing out, failing, or returning unexpected errors
- Users may experience interruptions, incomplete transactions, or inability to access key features

Immediate Action Required:
Please prioritize investigation and resolution of this issue immediately to minimize further impact on service availability and user experience.

Initial Troubleshooting Steps:
- Verify API health status and check for recent error spikes
- Review application and server logs for exceptions or failure patterns
- Confirm whether any recent deployments, configuration changes, or infrastructure updates may have triggered the issue
- Check authentication, database, and third-party service dependencies for failures
- Validate network connectivity, latency, and DNS resolution between impacted services
- Restart affected services if appropriate and monitor for recovery
- Escalate to engineering and infrastructure teams if the issue persists

This issue is considered high priority and requires immediate coordination across relevant teams until service is fully restored.

prompt_text: Write a newsletter introduction discussing global youth protests, focusing on their unique symbols and the impact on political movements.
reference_text:
The World: The activists and the anime
Plus, a U.S. government shutdown
View in browser|nytimes.com
October 1, 2025

By Katrin Bennhold

Good morning, world! From Nepal to Madagascar, Gen Z protesters are raging against the political establishment, with some success. These protests are taking place thousands of kilometers apart. But as my colleague Pranav Baskar reports, the protesters are linked by a pirate flag that might mystify their parents.

Also:

  • A U.S. government shutdown
  • What Moldova’s election says about Europe
  • Colombia’s Brayans fight back
Clockwise from top left: Protests in Nepal, Indonesia, the Philippines and Madagascar.  Clockwise from top left: Sunil Pradhan/Anadolu via Getty Images; Yasuyoshi Chiba/AFP; Ezra Acayan/Getty Images; Rijasolo/AFP

The activists and the anime

By Pranav Baskar

Gen Z protests are having a moment.

In just the last month, Nepal, Indonesia, the Philippines and Madagascar have all experienced large protests led by young people outraged over government corruption and inequality. Flying above each of the protests was a common flag: a grinning skull and bones wearing a straw hat.

The flag comes from a long-running Japanese manga and anime series called “One Piece,” which follows a scrappy crew of pirates as they take on a corrupt and repressive government. The franchise, recently relaunched as a live-action series on Netflix, has been dubbed in more than a dozen languages and has amassed a huge following, with more than 500 million copies of the print version published.

One of the first times the flag flew at protests was in 2023, at pro-Palestinian marches in Indonesia and Britain. In the years since, it has become a symbol for youth-led demonstrations around the world.

It hung on the gates of Nepal’s government complex, which was burned in protests that eventually toppled the government. It was painted on walls in Jakarta and hoisted by crowds in Manila. This week, it popped up in Madagascar, where protests forced the dissolution of the government on Monday.

“We know that Gen Z is protesting all around the world, and we wanted to use symbols that made sense to our generation,” Rakshya Bam, 26, one of the Nepali Gen Z protest organizers, told my colleague Hannah Beech. “The pirate flag, Jolly Roger, it’s like a common language now.”

The “One Piece” flag isn’t just an emblem — it’s an allegory. The protagonist, Luffy, is a terrorist or a freedom fighter, depending on whom you ask. His iconic straw hat was a gift from his childhood hero, who believed Luffy and his generation would eventually prevail.

The plot makes the symbol particularly resonant, another Nepali protester, Irfan Khan, told Hannah. “The pirate, it’s like a way to say we are not going to put up with injustice and corruption anymore,” he said.

It’s not the first time that protesters have found common cause with youth-coded cultural references. In the 2010s, protesters against a military coup in Thailand

message.content: From climate marches in Europe to pro-democracy demonstrations in Asia and racial justice movements in the Americas, youth-led protests are reshaping the political landscape with striking creativity and urgency. What sets many of these movements apart is their use of powerful symbols—handmade signs, songs, colors, gestures, and digital imagery—that turn protest into a shared language across borders. These symbols do more than capture attention; they build identity, strengthen solidarity, and help movements spread far beyond the streets where they begin. As young activists continue to challenge established power, their visual and cultural expressions are proving just as influential as their demands, leaving a lasting mark on political discourse and social change worldwide.

FYI - Customer complaint received:

Customer: First Works Co
Issue: Product specifications do not match requirements
Severity: 4/5

Please review and advise if escalation needed.

Alyssa Scholz

message.content:
Subject: Customer Complaint Regarding Product Specifications – Request for Evaluation

Team,

We have received a customer complaint indicating that the product specifications do not meet the required standards. The customer has raised concerns that certain aspects of the product may not align with the agreed-upon requirements.

Please review this issue carefully and conduct an evaluation of the product specifications against the stated requirements. We need to determine the cause of the discrepancy, assess any potential impact, and identify recommended corrective actions.

Kindly provide your findings and recommendations as soon as possible so we can respond to the customer promptly and appropriately.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

prompt_text: Write an email to a support team requesting an upgrade of digital marketing tools, including a detailed list of new tools and anticipated benefits.
reference_text: Hello Customer Support Team, I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out to enquire about the potential of upgrading our digital marketing software tools on our workstations. Our current tools are no longer suitable for our needs, and are significantly impacting the efficiency of our marketing campaigns. I believe that by modernizing these tools, we can improve our campaign performance and overall productivity. Could you kindly provide me with a detailed list of the latest available tools and the associated costs for these updates? Furthermore, I would greatly appreciate any insights or details about the anticipated benefits of these updates, such as increased efficiency, higher accuracy, and better customer interaction. I look forward to your prompt response and collaboration to enhance our digital marketing initiatives. I trust that with your assistance, we can elevate our campaigns to a new level and fulfill our marketing objectives. Please let me know if you need any additional information from me to proceed with this matter. I am excited to move forward and explore the possibilities these updates offer.

message.content:
Subject: Request for Upgrade of Digital Marketing Tools

Dear Support Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am writing to request an upgrade of our current digital marketing tools to better support our growing marketing needs and improve overall campaign performance. As our strategies become more data-driven and multi-channel, it is important that we equip the team with more advanced tools that can enhance efficiency, reporting, automation, and customer engagement.

Below is a detailed list of the tools we would like to add or upgrade, along with the anticipated benefits of each:

1. Marketing Automation Platform Upgrade
Current Need:
We need stronger automation capabilities for email workflows, lead nurturing, audience segmentation, and campaign scheduling.

Requested Upgrade:
A more advanced marketing automation platform such as HubSpot, Marketo, or ActiveCampaign.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Improved lead nurturing through automated customer journeys
- Better segmentation for personalized messaging
- Reduced manual work for campaign execution
- Higher conversion rates through timely follow-ups
- More detailed performance tracking across campaigns

2. SEO and Keyword Research Tool
Current Need:
Our existing SEO resources are limited and do not provide enough insight into keyword opportunities, backlinks, and competitor performance.

Requested Upgrade:
Tools such as SEMrush, Ahrefs, or Moz Pro.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Better keyword targeting for organic growth
- Improved competitor analysis
- Stronger backlink monitoring and strategy development
- More effective content optimization
- Increased website traffic from search engines

3. Social Media Management Platform
Current Need:
Managing multiple social media accounts manually is time-consuming and limits our ability to monitor engagement effectively.

Requested Upgrade:
A platform such as Hootsuite, Buffer, Sprout Social, or Later.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Centralized scheduling and publishing across channels
- Better tracking of audience engagement
- Improved response times to comments and messages
- Enhanced analytics for social performance
- More consistent brand presence online

4. Advanced Analytics and Reporting Tool
Current Need:
We need more comprehensive reporting to measure campaign performance, customer behavior, and return on investment.

Requested Upgrade:
Tools such as Google Analytics 4 enhancements, Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Clearer visibility into campaign effectiveness
- Better decision-making through real-time dashboards
- Easier reporting for stakeholders
- Improved attribution analysis
- Stronger understanding of customer journeys

5. Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Enhancement
Current Need:
Our current CRM setup could be improved to better align marketing and sales activities.

Requested Upgrade:
Enhancements or migration to platforms such as Salesforce, HubSpot CRM, or Zoho CRM.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Better alignment between marketing and sales teams
- Improved lead tracking and pipeline visibility
- More accurate customer data management
- Enhanced follow-up and engagement strategies
- Greater efficiency in managing prospects and customers

6. Content Creation and Design Tools
Current Need:
The team would benefit from more efficient content design and collaboration tools for creating marketing assets.

Requested Upgrade:
Tools such as Canva Pro, Adobe Creative Cloud, or Figma.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Faster creation of high-quality marketing materials
- Improved collaboration on creative projects
- More professional and consistent branding
- Greater flexibility for different content formats
- Reduced dependency on external design support

7. A/B Testing and Conversion Rate Optimization Tool
Current Need:
We need stronger capabilities to test landing pages, messaging, and user experience improvements.

Requested Upgrade:
Tools such as Optimizely, VWO, or Google Optimize alternatives.

Anticipated Benefits:
- Data-driven improvement of landing pages and campaigns
- Higher website conversion rates
- Better user experience through testing
- More effective messaging and design decisions
- Improved return on marketing spend

prompt_text: Draft an email to tech support detailing issues with a software deployment, including error messages and the urgency of the situation.
reference_text:
Hi Support,

Here's the information you requested:

* Error message: "Deployment failed. Please try again later."
* Mercury version: 1.2.5
* Account ID: 87654321

This issue is preventing me from meeting a critical deadline. Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,
John

message.content:
Subject: Urgent: Software Deployment Issues Requiring Immediate Assistance

Dear Tech Support,

I am reaching out to report several issues we are encountering with a recent software deployment and to request urgent assistance.

During the deployment process, we received multiple error messages, including:
- “Deployment failed: Unable to connect to server”
- “Error 503: Service unavailable”
- “Installation aborted due to missing dependencies”

These errors have prevented the deployment from completing successfully and are impacting our ability to proceed with critical operations. We have attempted to retry the deployment and verify the configuration, but the issue persists.

This situation is urgent, as the deployment is time-sensitive and the current failure is causing significant disruption to our workflow. Please investigate this issue as soon as possible and advise on the necessary steps to resolve it.

If you need any additional details, logs, or screenshots, I would be happy to provide them.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

message.content:
Subject: Action Required: Shipment on Hold Due to Pending Fees

Dear Customer,

We are contacting you regarding your shipment, which is currently on hold due to pending fees that must be paid before delivery can proceed.

To resolve this issue and release your shipment as quickly as possible, please follow these steps:

1. Review the pending charges associated with your shipment.
2. Submit payment using the available payment method provided in your shipment notice or billing communication.
3. Confirm that payment has been completed and retain your confirmation for reference.
4. Once payment is received and processed, your shipment will be released and forwarded for delivery.

Please note that processing times may vary slightly depending on the payment method used. To avoid additional delays, we recommend completing payment as soon as possible.

If you have any questions about the charges or need assistance with payment, please contact our customer support team. We are happy to help.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,  
Customer Service Team

prompt_text: Draft an urgent security alert from a bank to a customer, warning them about a potential security issue and advising immediate action to protect their account.

message.content: Urgent Security Alert: We’ve detected a potential security issue involving your bank account that may put your funds and personal information at risk. Please take immediate action to protect your account by logging in through our official website or mobile app to review recent activity, change your password, and verify your contact and security details. If you notice any unauthorized transactions or cannot access your account, contact our fraud team immediately using the number on the back of your card or on our official website. Do not share your password, PIN, or one-time verification codes with anyone.

message.content: Urgent: Our project management system has unexpectedly gone down due to server issues, and we need immediate technical support to restore functionality as soon as possible.

prompt_text: Create an informative email to bank customers about potential account restrictions due to security checks. Explain the steps needed to lift these restrictions and the importance of verifying their information.
reference_text: Dear Valued Customer Bank Of America is constantly working to increase security for all Online Banking users. To ensure the integrity of our online payment system, we periodically review accounts. Your account might be place on restricted status. Restricted accounts continue to receive payments, but they are limited in their ability to send or withdraw funds. To lift up this restriction, you need to login into your account (with your username or SSN and your password), then you have to complete our verification process. You must confirm your credit card details and your billing information as well. All restricted accounts have their billing information unconfirmed, meaning that you may no longer send money from your account until you have updated your billing information on file. To initiate the update confirmation process. Please follow the link below and fill in the necessary fields. Thank you Click here to continue Because your reply will not be transmitted via secure e-mail, the e-mail address that generated this alert will not accept replies. If you would like to contact Bank of America with questions or comments, please sign in to Online Banking and visit the customer service section.

message.content:
Subject: Important: Action Needed to Remove Security Restrictions on Your Account

Dear Customer,

To help protect your account and personal information, we may occasionally place temporary restrictions on certain account activities when additional security checks are required. These measures are designed to prevent unauthorized access and keep your finances secure.

If your account has been restricted, you may notice limited access to services such as online banking, transfers, withdrawals, or card transactions until the review is completed.

To help lift these restrictions as quickly as possible, please complete the following steps:

1. Verify your personal information  
   Confirm details such as your full name, address, date of birth, phone number, and email address.

2. Provide identification documents if requested  
   You may be asked to submit a valid government-issued ID and, in some cases, proof of address.

3. Review recent account activity  
   Check your recent transactions and report any activity you do not recognize immediately.

4. Contact our support team  
   If you have received a notification from us, please follow the instructions provided or contact customer service for assistance with the verification process.

Verifying your information promptly is important because it helps us:
- Protect your account from fraud and unauthorized use
- Confirm that your account details are accurate and up to date
- Restore full access to your banking services as quickly as possible

Please note that account restrictions are temporary and will be removed once the required checks have been successfully completed.

If you need assistance, please contact our customer support team through your usual banking channel.

Thank you for your prompt attention and for helping us keep your account secure.

Sincerely,  
[Bank Name]  
Customer Support Team

prompt_text: Draft a formal email to customer support highlighting an urgent data breach at a hospital, requesting immediate assistance and advice on securing compromised patient information.
reference_text: Dear Customer Support, I am contacting you to report a significant data breach that has affected our hospital recently. Our database has been compromised, exposing sensitive patient data. It is believed that the breach was caused by delayed updates to our security patches. We have already performed virus scans and updated our firewall settings, but we need urgent help to address this situation and prevent further breaches. Could you please provide us with the necessary steps to secure our database and offer any support available? We would greatly appreciate any additional measures we could take to minimize the impact and ensure future security. We await your prompt response. Best regards, [Your Name]

message.content:
Subject: Urgent Assistance Required Regarding Data Breach at Hospital

Dear Customer Support Team,

I am writing to report an urgent data breach involving our hospital’s systems, which may have compromised sensitive patient information. We have identified unauthorized access to confidential records, and this situation requires immediate attention to help contain the breach and protect affected data.

Given the seriousness of this incident, we request your immediate assistance in identifying the source and extent of the breach, securing our systems, and advising us on the necessary steps to prevent further unauthorized access. We would also appreciate guidance on best practices for safeguarding compromised patient information and ensuring compliance with all relevant data protection and healthcare privacy regulations.

Please treat this matter as a priority and respond as soon as possible with recommended next steps, available support options, and any urgent actions we should take while your team investigates.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this critical matter. We look forward to your immediate response.

Sincerely,  
[Your Full Name]  
[Your Position]  
[Hospital Name]  
[Contact Information]

message.content:
Subject: Action Required: Verify Recent Changes to Your Account Details

Dear [User Name],

We detected that some of your account details were recently changed by a third party. To protect your account and prevent any interruption in access, we need you to verify your identity as soon as possible.

Please complete the following steps:

1. Sign in to your account using your usual login credentials.
2. Review your recent account details and confirm whether you recognize the changes.
3. Verify your identity by following the instructions in the security verification section of your account.
4. Update your password immediately if you do not recognize the changes.
5. Enable two-factor authentication, if it is not already active.

Please note that failure to verify your identity promptly may result in temporary suspension of your account for security reasons.

If you believe your account has been accessed without your permission, contact our support team immediately at [Support Email/Phone Number].

Thank you for helping us keep your account secure.

Sincerely,  
[Company Name] Support Team

prompt_text: Write a newsletter article introducing a tool for comparing products based on their price-feature ratio, detailing how it helps consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

message.content:
**Compare Smarter: A Better Way to Measure Value Before You Buy**

When shopping for a new product, the lowest price doesn’t always mean the best deal. A budget option may lack important features, while a premium model may include extras you don’t actually need. That’s where a **price-feature ratio comparison tool** can make a real difference.

This tool helps consumers evaluate products by looking at **what they cost in relation to what they offer**. Instead of comparing price tags alone, it weighs features such as performance, durability, usability, warranty, and other key specifications to show which products deliver the most value for the money.

For shoppers, the biggest benefit is **clearer decision-making**. Rather than sorting through dozens of listings and trying to judge value manually, users can quickly see which products strike the best balance between affordability and functionality. This is especially useful in crowded categories like electronics, appliances, software, and subscription services, where feature sets can vary widely.

Another advantage is **greater confidence in purchases**. By highlighting products with strong value scores, the tool helps consumers avoid overpaying for unnecessary extras or choosing cheap options that may fall short in quality. It also makes it easier to identify “sweet spot” products—those that offer the most useful features at a reasonable price.

The tool can also support more personalized buying decisions. Users can prioritize the features that matter most to them, whether that’s battery life, storage capacity, customer support, or ease of use. This means comparisons are not just data-driven, but also tailored to individual needs and budgets.

In a marketplace full of choices, a price-feature ratio tool turns complex product research into a simpler, smarter process. For consumers who want to spend wisely and buy with confidence, it offers a practical way to focus on true value—not just cost.

reference_text:
Dear Customer Support,

I am inquiring about the data analytics tools your company employs to optimize investment portfolios for financial institutions. As a representative of a financial firm, I'm eager to learn more about tools and technologies that can aid in data-driven decision-making and enhancing investment strategies.

Can you provide details on the data analytics tools you offer, such as predictive modeling, machine learning, or data visualization? Additionally, I would appreciate sharing case studies or success stories illustrating how these tools have benefited other financial institutions in optimizing their investment portfolios.

I am also interested in the level of customization and support your company offers to its clients. Are the tools personalized to meet each firm's specific needs, or are they more standardized? What kind of support and training do you provide to ensure clients maximize the tools' potential?

I'm particularly keen to understand how these tools can aid in risk management, asset allocation, and performance measurement within our investment portfolios. Are there any specific features or functionalities that are tailored to these areas?

I would greatly appreciate a detailed response, including relevant examples or case studies. I look forward to your reply and to learning about how your company can support our investment optimization efforts.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Chris Haun wrote:

Ken Coar, Sanagendamgagwedweinini  http://Golux.Com/coar/
Author, developer, opinionist      http://Apache-Server.Com/

"Millennium hand and shrimp!"

message.content:
Alex: We need to talk about the future of the station. FM is great, but moving to an internet radio platform feels inevitable.

Jordan: I agree, but “moving online” sounds cheaper and easier than it actually is. Everyone assumes we just plug in a laptop and start streaming.

Alex: Exactly. The first issue is equipment. We can’t rely on our current setup alone. We’d need a reliable streaming encoder, better computers, audio interfaces, and probably upgrades to the board if we want the online sound quality to be consistent.

Jordan: And then there’s software. Some streaming platforms charge monthly fees, automation software can get expensive, and licensing is a whole separate headache.

Alex: Licensing is probably the part most students don’t think about. Broadcasting over FM under the college’s existing agreements is one thing, but internet streaming can require additional royalty payments and reporting.

Jordan: Right, and if we want to archive shows or offer on-demand content, that can add even more legal and financial complications.

Alex: Then there’s bandwidth. If fifty people tune in, that’s manageable. If five hundred tune in during a big campus event, suddenly we need enough server capacity to handle all those listeners without the stream crashing.

Jordan: Which means either paying for a hosting provider that can scale or asking the college IT department for serious support. Neither option is exactly free.

Alex: Plus we’d need a proper website or app integration. If the stream is hard to find or constantly buffering, people will give up immediately.

Jordan: And maintaining that site means ongoing costs too—domain registration, web hosting, security updates, and probably someone with actual web development skills.

Alex: Staffing is another challenge. Our DJs know how to run a live board, but streaming brings in new responsibilities: metadata management, remote broadcasting tools, podcast editing, social media promotion.

Jordan: Training alone could take a semester. And because students graduate, we’d have to keep retraining new staff every year.

Alex: The upside is huge, though. We wouldn’t just be broadcasting to people within range of campus. Alumni, parents, and prospective students could listen from anywhere.

Jordan: That’s the strongest argument for it. Internet radio gives us a bigger audience and more flexibility, but it turns the station into more of a digital media operation than just a traditional radio station.

Alex: So the real question isn’t whether it’s possible. It’s whether we can afford the startup costs and sustain the ongoing expenses.

Jordan: Exactly. Going online isn’t a one-time purchase. It’s a long-term commitment—technical, financial, and organizational.

Alex: Maybe the best approach is a phased transition. Keep FM running, start with a basic stream, and expand once we know the actual costs.

Jordan: That makes sense. If we pitch it that way to student government, they might be more willing to fund a pilot program instead of a full overhaul.

Alex: And if the pilot proves there’s audience demand, we’d have real numbers to justify more investment.

Jordan: Which is probably the only way this happens. Passion gets the station started, but budgets keep it alive.

prompt_text: Create a detailed report discussing the challenges of using email filters for detecting spam, including an analysis of false positives and the role of MIME and HTML structures.

message.content:
Email filtering remains one of the most widely used defenses against spam, yet it is inherently difficult to make highly accurate. Spam is not a static target: attackers continuously adapt their wording, formatting, sender infrastructure, and message structure to bypass detection systems. As a result, email filters must balance competing goals—blocking malicious or unwanted messages while ensuring that legitimate communication is delivered reliably. This tension is especially visible in the problem of false positives, where valid email is incorrectly classified as spam, and in the technical complexity introduced by MIME and HTML, which can obscure message intent and content.

A central challenge in spam detection is the adversarial nature of the problem. Traditional rule-based filters often rely on indicators such as suspicious keywords, malformed headers, unusual sender domains, or known spam signatures. However, spammers can easily modify these features. They may rotate domains, alter text slightly, embed content in images, or use benign-looking language. Machine learning approaches improve adaptability by learning statistical patterns from large datasets, but they introduce their own difficulties. Models can become outdated as spam campaigns evolve, and they may learn correlations that do not generalize well. For example, a model might associate certain marketing language, formatting patterns, or geographic sources with spam, even when these characteristics also appear in legitimate newsletters or transactional emails.

False positives are among the most serious operational problems in email filtering. A false positive occurs when a legitimate message is blocked, quarantined, or sent to a junk folder. While false negatives—spam that reaches the inbox—are annoying, false positives can be more damaging because they interfere with real communication. Important business correspondence, password reset messages, invoices, medical notifications, legal notices, or personal emails may be delayed or missed entirely. In enterprise settings, even a small false positive rate can affect a large number of messages and create significant workflow disruption. Users often lose trust in the email system if they must routinely search spam folders for legitimate mail.

The causes of false positives are varied. Legitimate senders sometimes use language, formatting, or delivery practices that resemble spam. Marketing emails are a common example: they may contain promotional phrases, links, tracking parameters, and HTML-heavy layouts that overlap with known spam characteristics. New or low-reputation domains can also be penalized by reputation-based systems, even when they belong to valid organizations. Similarly, messages sent through shared infrastructure may inherit poor reputations because other senders on the same IP range have abused the service. Authentication failures or misconfigurations in SPF, DKIM, or DMARC can further increase the likelihood that legitimate mail is treated suspiciously, even when the content itself is harmless.

Another challenge is that filtering decisions are often probabilistic rather than absolute. Modern systems combine many signals—content analysis, sender reputation, authentication checks, behavioral patterns, attachment types, URL reputation, and user feedback. Each signal may be weak in isolation, but together they produce a confidence score. The threshold chosen for blocking or quarantining messages directly affects the balance between false positives and false negatives. A more aggressive filter catches more spam but also risks blocking more legitimate mail. A more permissive filter reduces collateral damage but allows more spam through. This tradeoff is difficult to optimize universally because user expectations and risk tolerance vary across environments.

MIME plays a significant role in the complexity of spam detection. MIME, or Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions, allows email to contain multiple parts such as plain text, HTML, inline images, file attachments, and alternative representations of the same content. While MIME is essential for modern email functionality, it also gives attackers many ways to hide or fragment malicious content. A spam message may place benign text in one part and the actual payload in another, or it may exploit nested multipart structures to confuse parsers and filters.

Dear Technical Support Team,

We have identified that monitoring alerts are not being triggered as expected, which has created a significant gap in our ability to detect and respond to potential issues in real time.

prompt_text: Describe a technical issue affecting a data analytics dashboard and outline the initial steps taken to resolve it, including server actions and database considerations.
reference_text: The Data Analytics Dashboard is stationary. It might be because of excessive load on the Cassandra database. I restarted the server and reviewed the latest GitHub updates.

message.content: The data analytics dashboard experienced a technical issue where reports and visualizations failed to load correctly, likely due to backend service instability or delayed database responses. Initial troubleshooting involved checking the application server status, reviewing error logs, and restarting affected services to restore normal operation. On the database side, the team examined query performance, verified connection health, and checked for locking or resource saturation that might have slowed data retrieval. These early steps helped isolate whether the problem originated from the server layer, the database layer, or the interaction between both.

- A description of the crash behavior
- Exact error messages returned
- Affected endpoints or services
- Frequency of the crashes
- Whether the crashes are reproducible

3. Logs and diagnostics
- Relevant application logs
- API/server error logs
- Stack traces
- Timestamp(s) of recent crash incidents
- Correlation IDs or request IDs, if available

4. Environment details
- Production, staging, or development environment
- OS/runtime version
- Framework version
- Deployment method
- Hosting platform or infrastructure details

5. Recent system changes
- Recent deployments or releases
- Dependency/package updates
- Configuration changes
- Infrastructure or networking changes
- Authentication, gateway, or load balancer changes

6. Impact assessment
- Number of users or requests affected
- Severity of the outage
- Any temporary workaround in place

7. Reproduction and troubleshooting
- Steps to reproduce
- Whether rollback was attempted
- Whether disabling the SDK stops the crashes
- Any monitoring or alerting data showing when the issue began

If possible, send the details in this format:

- SDK:
- Version:
- Environment:
- Crash start time:
- Error message:
- Logs:
- Recent changes:
- Reproduction steps:
- Mitigations tried:
- Current impact:
